title: CYLINDER HEAD — Inspection (Cont'd) — VALVE SEAT REPLACEMENT

figures_detected.description: Diagram showing recess diameter (top and bottom) in cylinder head for valve seat replacement
Illustration showing cutting or grinding of valve seat using a suitable tool with both hands
Illustration showing application of fine grinding compound and lapping valve against its seat
Illustration showing valve seating condition check; W dimension refers to SDS

content_blocks.title: Reaming bore for service valve seat

steps.text: Bore out old seat until it collapses. Set machine depth stop so that boring cannot contact bottom face of seat recess in cylinder head.
Ream the cylinder head recess diameter and depth.
notes: Reaming bore for service valve seat — Unit: mm (in). Intake recess diameter: 37.922 - 37.947 (1.4930 - 1.4941), depth: 8.017 - 8.167 (0.3156 - 0.3215). Exhaust recess diameter: 32.422 - 32.447 (1.2765 - 1.2774), depth: 8.30 - 8.45 (0.327 - 0.333).

steps.text: Heat cylinder head to a temperature of 150 to 160°C (302 to 320°F) and press fit seat until it seats on the bottom.
notes: Use the valve guide center for reaming to ensure valve seat will have the correct fit.
When replacing valve seat, valve should be replaced as well.

steps.text: Cut or grind valve seat using a suitable tool at the specified dimensions as shown in SDS (EM-5047).
notes: The cutting should be done with both hands to obtain a uniform and concentric finish.

steps.text: Apply a small amount of fine grinding compound to the valve's contacting face and put the valve into its guide. Lap valve against its seat until proper valve seating is obtained.
